steps.title: Turn the charges into two points
steps.content: Use a Desmos table instead of doing the fee algebra by hand. The first two hours cost 160 dollars, so the model passes through $(2,160)$. Six hours cost 360 dollars, so it also passes through $(6,360)$. Since the choices are linear, fit those two points with $y_1\sim m x_1+b$.

steps.title: Read the line from Desmos
steps.content: The regression gives $m=50$ and $b=60$, so the total-cost model is $$f(x)=50x+60.$$ The slope means each additional hour adds 50 dollars, and the intercept is what makes the line pass through the two-hour base charge.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice A is $f(x)=50x+60$. It gives $f(2)=160$ and $f(6)=360$, matching both facts in the question. Choice C also gives $360$ at $x=6$, but it gives only $120$ at $x=2$, so it fails the first-two-hours charge. Choice A is correct.
